## Bug catalog

### wave-1-agent-3-bug-1 (P1, missing_requirement)
**Title:** SmithingTemplateTrades does not register trader trades on 26.1.2 (Fabric trade API removed)

**File:** `src/main/kotlin/com/dndweapons/trade/SmithingTemplateTrades.kt`

**Evidence:** On 26.1.2 the function logs a notice and returns early; the
fabric-object-builder-api-v1 23.0.13+b61fef4359 (the version bundled with
fabric-api 0.144.0+26.1 for MC 26.1.2) no longer contains TradeOfferHelper.class
-- verified by inspecting the jar contents.

**Expected:** Per cycle-5 acceptance criteria Task 3, trader trades should work on
all 5 versions including 26.1.2.

**Suggested fix:** Either (a) accept graceful degradation as implemented
(templates remain obtainable via crafting and stronghold/bastion loot, which DO
work on 26.1.2), or (b) implement a data-driven trade JSON in
`src/main/resources/data/.../trade` for 26.1.2 since the Fabric helper is gone.

### wave-1-agent-4-bug-1 (P2, missing_requirement)
**Title:** Recipe canonicalization is not single-form across all 5 MC versions
because vanilla codecs differ

**File:** `src/main/resources/data/dndweapons/recipe/longsword_diamond.json`

**Evidence:** MC 1.21.1 Ingredient codec requires `{ item: ... }` / `{ tag: ... }`
object form; MC 1.21.4+ HolderSet codec requires bare string
(`'minecraft:item'` / `'#namespace:tag'`) and rejects the object form. Per-jar
codec inspection confirmed both formats are mutually exclusive.

**Expected:** Single canonical item-ref format that works on all 5 MC versions.

**Suggested fix:** Implemented mitigation: shared `recipe/` uses 1.21.4+
canonical form. Per-version overlay at
`versions/1.21.1/src/main/resources/data/dndweapons/recipe/` (58 files) provides
object-form recipes for 1.21.1 via `processResources duplicatesStrategy=EXCLUDE`
first-wins. Accept the mitigation -- a single canonical form is provably
impossible given Mojang's codec rewrite at 1.21.4.

### wave-1-agent-4-bug-2 (P2, missing_requirement)
**Title:** 1.20.1 has no smithing recipe overlay; 1.20.1 cannot craft tiered
weapons via smithing table

**File:** `src/main/resources/data/dndweapons/recipe/`

**Evidence:** 1.20.1 reads recipe JSONs from `data/<ns>/recipes/` (plural) while
the shared smithing files live in `data/<ns>/recipe/` (singular). No 1.20.1
overlay was generated for the 56 smithing recipes.

**Expected:** 1.20.1 should also support smithing-table crafting of the tier
upgrades, per Phase 4 acceptance.

**Suggested fix:** Generate 56 per-version 1.20.1 overlay files under
`versions/1.20.1/src/main/resources/data/dndweapons/recipes/` (plural directory)
with the 1.20.1 format: result uses `{item: ..., count: 1}` (not `{id: ...}`),
all ingredients use `{ tag: ... }` / `{ item: ... }` object form.

### wave-2-agent-1-bug-1 (P1, missing_requirement)
**Title:** Git tag NOT applied; chiseledBuild/chiseledTest/chiseledRunGametest
not verified by orchestrator session

**File:** `docs/superpowers/plans/phase-4-verification-final.md`

**Evidence:** Verification doc status: HOLD. The orchestrator session cannot run
`./gradlew chiseledBuild` and therefore cannot honestly confirm GREEN status on
the 5 MC versions. Per Anthropic guidance, claiming verification without evidence
is forbidden.

**Expected:** Per Task 9 / wave-2 acceptance: `./gradlew chiseledBuild` green on
all 5 versions; `./gradlew chiseledTest` green on all 5 versions;
`./gradlew chiseledRunGametest` green on all 5 versions; git tag
`phase-4-smithing-ladder` applied and pushed.

**Suggested fix:** User runs `./gradlew chiseledBuild` locally. If GREEN, runs
`chiseledTest` and `chiseledRunGametest`. If all GREEN, applies the tag with
`git tag phase-4-smithing-ladder && git push origin phase-4-smithing-ladder` and
updates the verification doc status from HOLD to DONE.

## Convergence note

This was cycle 6. Cycle 5 had 18 bugs (8 P0 + 6 P1 + 2 P2 + 2 P3). Cycle 6
reduced this to 4 bugs (0 P0 + 2 P1 + 2 P2 + 0 P3) -- a 77% reduction.

The 8 P0 compilation failures from cycle 5 are all fixed via stonecutter version
gates and corrected API references. The remaining issues are NOT compilation
blockers; they are graceful-degradation decisions or build-verification gaps that
require user-side gradle execution to resolve.
